summary: The diff implements all five requirements comprehensively. The backend Motoko actor (REQ-1), frontend topic management (REQ-2), due reminders section (REQ-3), study growth chart (REQ-4), and visual theme (REQ-5) are all present. One notable issue: the backend's markReminderDone re-adds a reminder with a fresh timestamp rather than simply removing it. The required hero image is referenced in App.tsx but not included in the diff as an added file. The data model uses var variables but not stable variables, which may violate the persistence constraint.

missing_requirements.requirement: Persist all data in stable Motoko variables
missing_requirements.reason: The backend/main.mo uses plain var topics, var reminders, and var studyLogs instead of stable var declarations. Without stable, data will be lost on canister upgrades, violating the constraint that all data must be persisted in stable Motoko variables.

missing_requirements.requirement: A minimalist book-and-growth-chart illustration (study-hero.dim_800x400.png) must be generated and present as an image asset
missing_requirements.reason: The image is referenced in App.tsx at /assets/generated/study-hero.dim_800x400.png but no such image file appears in the diff's added files list.

hallucinated_features.description: markReminderDone re-adds the reminder with a fresh timestamp after marking it done, effectively rescheduling it for the same interval again
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asks to mark the specified interval as completed and log the event. Re-inserting the reminder with a new timestamp creates unintended re-scheduling behavior that was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Automatic 60-second polling for due reminders via refetchInterval
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request non-goals explicitly state no automatic background polling daemon and that user-triggered refresh is sufficient. A manual refresh button is present but the automatic 60-second interval polling was not requested.
